name: P. aeruginosa sensor (PQS/PqsR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects Pseudomonas aeruginosa via its quinolone signal (PQS). The PqsR (MvfR) receptor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible pigment, in the safe Pseudomonas relative P. putida KT2440.

input.detects: The PQS quorum signal secreted by P. aeruginosa (complements its AHL signal)

sensing.sensorMechanism: PqsR (MvfR) binds PQS and activates the pqsA promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: PQS -> PqsR activates PpqsA -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> CRISPRi represses a constitutive amilCP cassette -> pigment fades when the pathogen signal is present (NOT). Pair an inverter for colour-on.

parts.name: PqsR (MvfR) receptor
parts.notes: PQS-responsive LysR-family activator; works in the related P. putida host.

output.result: Colour change indicates P. aeruginosa PQS in the sample.

performance.notes: Senses the pathogen's signal molecule, not the live organism; PqsR and CRISPRi are validated separately and integration is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: Safe P. putida reads a secreted small molecule; no P. aeruginosa culture required.
safety.notes: Indirect, safe detection in a related non-pathogenic Pseudomonas.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PqsR + PpqsA -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP. SEVA vectors.

buildSteps.title: Transform P. putida KT2440
buildSteps.description: Electroporate; select; confirm baseline pigment without PQS.

buildSteps.title: Add sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the filtered sample + PQS standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the P. aeruginosa PqsR/PQS quorum module with P. putida CRISPRi
partsSources: Pesci et al. / PQS quorum sensing (P. aeruginosa)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
